channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US singer D4VD accused of stabbing and killing a 14-year-old girl
item.description: According to prosecutors, American singer D4VD has been charged with the brutal stabbing and dismemberment of a 14-year-old girl. Authorities allege that the singer attacked the girl multiple times, r...

item.title: Larry Lamb, 78, discusses confronting mortality through poetry after semi-retirement from acting
item.description: Larry Lamb, the 78-year-old actor known for roles in *EastEnders* and *Gavin & Stacey*, has opened up about how returning to poetry has helped him reflect on his own mortality. After taking a step bac...

item.title: University Students Recreate Sir William Hamilton's 18th-Century Mechanical Volcano
item.description: Two engineering students at the University of Melbourne have successfully recreated a mechanical volcano originally envisioned by Sir William Hamilton in 1775. Drawing from Hamilton's preserved sketch...

item.title: Assam Assembly Elections 2026: Himanta Sarma Seeks Second Term Amid Anti-Incumbency Challenges
item.description: The upcoming Assam assembly elections on May 4, 2026, have placed Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma and the BJP-led NDA under intense scrutiny. If the NDA secures a win, it will mark a hat-trick of t...

item.title: Bassilyo wins 500,000 pesos on Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal
item.description: Filipino rapper Bassilyo showcased his luck and strategy on the popular game show 'Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal,' ultimately winning a prize of 500,000 pesos. The show, hosted by Luis Manzano, featured c...

item.title: Crowdfunding Initiative Aims to Boost Rural Communities' Economy
item.description: The Chartered Institute for Business Accountants (Ciba) has introduced a crowdfunding platform to integrate rural communities into South Africa's mainstream economy. This initiative aims to gather R10...

item.title: Pakistan Faces Health Crisis from Reused and Falsely Labelled Syringes
item.description: The Pakistan Medical Association (PMA) has raised a serious alarm about a potential health crisis due to the continued use of reusable syringes despite a nationwide ban. The PMA highlighted that syrin...

item.title: Taylor Swift Files Trademarks Amid Growing Celebrity Deepfake Scams on TikTok
item.description: Taylor Swift has filed three trademark applications to protect her image and voice, including a famous photograph from her Eras tour and two sound marks for the phrases “Hey, it’s Taylor Swift” and “H...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Revenue Association requests government to ease workload and form new Pay Revision Commission
item.description: In Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh, the Revenue Services Association has formally urged the state government to take immediate steps to reduce the workload of Tahsildars and other revenue staff. The new co...

item.title: High Park Cherry Blossoms Reach Peak Bloom, Drawing Crowds and Wildlife Concerns
item.description: Every spring, High Park in Toronto becomes a hub for cherry blossom enthusiasts as tens of thousands of visitors gather to witness the fleeting beauty of the blossoms. This year, the blossoms reached ...

item.title: Older Parents Reflect on the Challenges of Waiting for Grandchildren
item.description: Amanda Craig explores the poignant experience of many older parents who find themselves longing for grandchildren after raising their own children. She describes the sense of 'groodiness'—a blend of b...

item.title: Australia updates teen social media rules amid widespread underage use
item.description: The Australian federal government has recently revised its controversial teen social media ban, initially introduced in December 2025. The policy, intended to restrict Australians under 16 from access...

item.title: US to transfer Gaza aid and monitoring role to International Stabilisation Force
item.description: WASHINGTON: The United States is reportedly planning to close its Civil-Military Coordination Centre (CMCC) near Gaza, a body that has faced criticism for failing to effectively monitor the Israel-Ham...

item.title: Nyandarua Farmer Builds Modern Cowshed with Tiles and Music for Comfort
item.description: In Nyandarua, a local TikToker shared a video of a high-end cowshed that has left Kenyans both amazed and amused. The dairy project, owned by Peter Kamau, features fully tiled feeding troughs, spaciou...

item.title: Gov’t enlists fisherfolk for sea monitoring, provides aid amid Balikatan exercises
item.description: The Philippine government is working with local fisherfolk to help monitor nearby waters amid the ongoing Balikatan military exercises. Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. emphasized that fishermen...

item.title: University of Waterloo student maps unusual and historic highway features across Ontario
item.description: A first-year civil engineering student at the University of Waterloo has created an online map showcasing unusual and little-known features across Ontario’s highway network. Jonathan Silverman, origin...

item.title: Shahzada House: Legacy of Bukhara’s Exiled Prince in Abbottabad
item.description: Shahzada House in Abbottabad stands as a quiet testament to the exile of Mir Syed Abdul Malik Tura, a Bukhara prince who resisted his father’s alliance with the Russians in the 19th century. After his...

item.title: Governor Bala Holds Stakeholders Meeting Amid PDP Internal Issues
item.description: Governor Bala Mohammed dey hold important meeting with Bauchi State PDP members as the party dey face wahala from inside. According to reports, the meeting dey involve wide stakeholders, and na major ...

item.title: House Justice Panel Confident of Securing Votes to Impeach VP Sara Duterte
item.description: A member of the House Justice Committee, Representative Terry Ridon of Bicol Saro Partylist, expressed confidence that the lower chamber can obtain sufficient votes to impeach Vice President Sara Dute...

item.title: Albanese Government’s Revised Big Tech Levy Faces Challenges in Media Classification
item.description: The Albanese government’s updated approach to the news media bargaining code, dubbed 'News Media Code 2: Bargaining Wars,' aims to better support journalism in Australia. The initiative imposes a 2.25...
